title: Customizing MOON
text: simplest edits. discount_rate, cost_currency, cohort_n single-cell slots. Setting cohort_n 1 makes $trace$n per-capita probability mass rather head-count. model behaviour unchanged — cohort_n purely denominator.
code: params <- moon_params_norway("female")  params$discount_rate <- 0 params$cohort_n <- c(female = 1L) # turn head-counts into proportions  res <- moon_deterministic(params)  c(LE = summary(res)$LE, total_disc = summary(res)$total_cost_disc) LE   total_disc     81.49437 116820.15441

title: Customizing MOON
text: transition_probs data frame one row per age start_age:(max_age - 1L) one column per transition (NW_OW, OW_NW, OW_OB1, OB1_OW, OB1_OB2, OB2_OB1). Halve every yearly NW→OW probability childhood ages : Compare untouched baseline stitching two long frames:  applies wholesale replacement — assign new vector length: validator catch --range value skip pmin() guard: age column fixed: transition_probs$age must exactly start_age:(max_age - 1L). want shrink simulation horizon, change start_age / max_age slice transition_probs match — see §6 coupled case init_prev.
code: params <- moon_params_norway("female") idx <- params$transition_probs$age <= 17 params$transition_probs$NW_OW[idx] <-   params$transition_probs$NW_OW[idx] * 0.5  res_low <- moon_deterministic(params) res_base <- moon_deterministic(moon_params_norway("female"))  prev_base <- transform(   moon_prevalence(res_base, "alive"),   scenario = "Baseline" ) prev_low <- transform(   moon_prevalence(res_low, "alive"),   scenario = "Halved NW->OW (ages 2-17)" ) prev <- rbind(prev_base, prev_low) prev <- prev[prev$state %in% c("N_always", "N_prev", "OW", "OB1", "OB2"), ]  ggplot(   prev,   aes(x = age, y = prevalence, colour = state, linetype = scenario) ) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +   labs(     x = "Age",     y = "Prevalence (% of alive)",     title = "Effect of halving childhood NW->OW transitions",     colour = "State",     linetype = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() params$transition_probs$OB1_OB2 <-   pmin(params$transition_probs$OB1_OB2 * 1.25, 1) moon_check_params() found 1 problem(s):   - `transition_probs$OB1_OB2` contains values outside [0, 1] or non-finite.

title: Customizing MOON
text: mortality_hr three-row data frame keyed age band (age_lower = c(35, 50, 70)) columns OW, OB1, OB2. validator pins band structure: can change values, number position bands. useful sensitivity: decouple BMI mortality entirely (set every HR 1): gap cohort-level life-expectancy attributable BMI-mortality coupling.
code: params <- moon_params_norway("female") params$mortality_hr$OW <- 1 params$mortality_hr$OB1 <- 1 params$mortality_hr$OB2 <- 1  c(   LE_baseline = summary(moon_deterministic(moon_params_norway("female")))$LE,   LE_no_hr = summary(moon_deterministic(params))$LE ) LE_baseline    LE_no_hr    81.49437    82.92830

what: 8. Bringing your own data via data_dir=
title: Customizing MOON
text: moon_params_norway() reads CSVs data_dir, defaults bundled inst/extdata/ directory. Point directory files filenames schemas, can swap entire parameter set without touching R. Expected layout. Cost transition filenames sex-keyed configurable; life table file name configurable via lifetable_file = (defaulting "Lifetable_Norway_2017.csv"): CSV State value "N" renamed "NW" read; transition labels source files use N_OW / OW_N / etc. renamed engine names (NW_OW / OW_NW) load time. bundled inst/extdata/ directory installed package canonical schema; copy starting point:

title: Customizing MOON
text: moon_params_norway("...", uncertainty = TRUE) returns shape deterministic loader uncertain slots replaced moon_param_* spec objects. customize one , build new spec assign back list-column. worked example: OW mortality HR ages 35–50 published 95% CI [1.15, 1.20]. Suppose want widen conservative [1.05, 1.30]: ’s ’s needed — moon_psa(spec, ...) pick new spec next time samples.
code: spec <- moon_params_norway("female", uncertainty = TRUE)  spec$mortality_hr$OW[[1]] <- moon_param_lognormal(   point = 1.17,   lower = 1.05,   upper = 1.30 )  # Quick sanity check on the new spec moon_param_value(spec$mortality_hr$OW[[1]]) [1] 1.17 set.seed(1) moon_param_sample(spec$mortality_hr$OW[[1]], n = 5) [1] 1.130740 1.181765 1.117927 1.276242 1.191194

title: Customizing MOON
text: constructors: Every spec implements two generics: moon_param_value(x) — deterministic point estimate. Used safety net spec encountered deterministic context. moon_param_sample(x, n, ...) — n random draws. Drop fully-custom Dirichlet init_prev spec ’ve moved “fixed entry distribution” “uncertain entry distribution”: (Note: moon_psa() currently treat init_prev uncertain published model doesn’t — see moon_sample_params() source want extend sampler.)
code: fix <- moon_param_fixed(1.5) ln <- moon_param_lognormal(point = 1.45, lower = 1.30, upper = 1.62) gam <- moon_param_gamma(mean_vec = c(0, 100, 250), se_vec = c(0, 30, 60)) dir <- moon_param_dirichlet(c(NW = 90, OW = 9, OB1 = 0.7, OB2 = 0.3))  set.seed(42) moon_param_value(ln) [1] 1.45 moon_param_sample(ln, n = 5) [1] 1.566003 1.404754 1.479862 1.502441 1.483284 moon_param_value(dir) NW    OW   OB1   OB2 0.900 0.090 0.007 0.003 moon_param_sample(dir, n = 3) NW         OW         OB1          OB2 [1,] 0.8906569 0.08371422 0.017872406 0.0077564222 [2,] 0.9227156 0.06227943 0.011955971 0.0030490462 [3,] 0.9494967 0.04273105 0.007001911 0.0007703877 spec$init_prev <- moon_param_dirichlet(   c(NW = 89.8, OW = 8.98, OB1 = 0.86, OB2 = 0.33) )

title: Customizing MOON
text: moon_psa() two correlation toggles: correlate_hr = TRUE — share one rnorm() draw vector across lognormal HR specs iteration. legacy “single-Z” convention original Excel implementation: high-OW iterations also high-OB1 high-OB2. correlate_cost = TRUE — share one runif() draw across gamma cost specs iteration (“single-U”). High-cost iterations high across every state-age cell jointly. default TRUE match published runs. Set FALSE independent draws per spec — useful ’re investigating much credible-interval width comes correlation assumption .
code: spec <- moon_params_norway("female", uncertainty = TRUE)  psa_corr <- moon_psa(spec, n_iter = 100, seed = 1, correlate_hr = TRUE) psa_indp <- moon_psa(spec, n_iter = 100, seed = 1, correlate_hr = FALSE)

what: 1. The MOON model in one screen
title: Getting started with MOON
text: MOON discrete-time Markov cohort model simulates Norwegian birth cohort age 2 ~age 100, 1-year cycles. state space five live BMI bands plus absorbing dead state: N_always — always normal weight (BMI 18.5–25) N_prev — currently normal weight previously OW OB OW — overweight (BMI 25–30) OB1 — obese grade 1 (BMI 30–35) OB2 — obese grade 2+ (BMI ≥ 35) dead — absorbing Transition probabilities come parametric survival analyses Norwegian longitudinal cohorts; mortality age- BMI-specific (life table × hazard ratios Global BMI Mortality Collaboration); costs per-capita 2009 EUR/year age state. original model implemented Excel — see Bjørnelv et al. 2021, Medical Decision Making, doi:10.1177/0272989X20971589. R port lifts every input typed objects, adds S3 layer run output, exposes clean PSA path parameter-spec system.

title: Getting started with MOON
text: moon_deterministic list four named slots: $trace — long data frame (age, sex, state, n). Six states including dead engine’s full N_always / N_prev split. n head-count denominated params$cohort_n. $costs — long data frame (age, sex, state, cost, cost_disc), five live states. cost total euros age-state cell incurred; cost_disc applies params$discount_rate. dead excluded. $params — input list, stored verbatim. Useful auditing re-running. $meta — runtime metadata (R version, timestamp, duration, horizon, discount rate, applied tp_overrides).

title: Getting started with MOON
text: worked example: take full cohort decomposition moon_prevalence() turn stacked-area plot — every state one axis, summing 100% initial cohort every age.  never locked canned plots. extractors $trace / $costs frames give raw long data; everything else whatever downstream code best.
code: state_mix <- moon_prevalence(res_F, denominator = "initial") state_mix$state <- factor(   state_mix$state,   levels = c("dead", "N_always", "N_prev", "OW", "OB1", "OB2") )  state_palette <- c(   dead     = "grey70",   N_always = "#1b7837",   N_prev   = "#7fbf7b",   OW       = "#f1a340",   OB1      = "#e08214",   OB2      = "#b35806" )  ggplot(state_mix, aes(x = age, y = prevalence, fill = state)) +   geom_area(position = "stack", colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +   scale_y_continuous(     labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),     expand = c(0, 0)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = state_palette) +   labs(     x = "Age",     y = "Share of initial cohort",     fill = NULL,     title = "Female cohort state composition — Norwegian baseline",     subtitle = "Stack reaches 100% of the age-2 cohort at every age"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Getting started with MOON
text: moon_params_norway() single-sex per call. Run three strata stitch headline numbers one table: lifetime cumulative incremental costs per cohort member, relative -NW counterfactual.
code: res_M <- moon_deterministic(moon_params_norway("male")) res_both <- moon_deterministic(moon_params_norway("both"))  inc_cost_per_capita <- function(result) {   m <- merge(     result$trace,     result$costs,     by = c("age", "sex", "state"),     all.x = TRUE   )   m$c <- ifelse(m$n > 0 & !is.na(m$cost), m$cost / m$n, 0)   c_NW <- m$c[m$state == "N_always"]   names(c_NW) <- m$age[m$state == "N_always"]   m$c_NW <- c_NW[as.character(m$age)]   obese <- m$state %in% c("OW", "OB1", "OB2")   sum(m$n[obese] * (m$c[obese] - m$c_NW[obese])) /     unname(result$params$cohort_n) }  data.frame(   sex = c("female", "male", "both"),   inc_cost_eur = round(c(     inc_cost_per_capita(res_F),     inc_cost_per_capita(res_M),     inc_cost_per_capita(res_both)   )) ) sex inc_cost_eur 1 female        16105 2   male         4092 3   both         9604

title: Getting started with MOON
text: Every paper scenario can run passing one-shot tp_overrides list moon_deterministic(). Three slots recognised: set_zero — character vector transition columns zero (e.g. "OW_OB1", closes path obesity ladder). init_prev — replace params’ initial state vector run. start_age — start cohort later params$start_age (used paper scenarios SA3-6, start everyone age 30). example reproduces paper scenario SA2 (eliminate OB1+OB2 closing OW→OB1 re-allocating starting OB1+OB2 mass OW): YLL_female per-person years--life lost attributable obesity cohort — gap obesity-eliminated counterfactual baseline run. tp_overrides= one-shot flag passed call site; params object untouched. instead want permanent change (“new baseline”), edit params directly — see “Customizing MOON” vignette.
code: ip <- params_F$init_prev ip_sa2 <- c(   NW = unname(ip["NW"]),   OW = unname(ip["OW"] + ip["OB1"] + ip["OB2"]),   OB1 = 0,   OB2 = 0 )  res_F_sa2 <- moon_deterministic(   params_F,   tp_overrides = list(set_zero = "OW_OB1", init_prev = ip_sa2) )  c(   LE_baseline = summary(res_F)$LE,   LE_SA2 = summary(res_F_sa2)$LE,   YLL_female = summary(res_F_sa2)$LE - summary(res_F)$LE ) LE_baseline      LE_SA2  YLL_female   81.494375   82.807107    1.312732

title: Getting started with MOON
text: PSA uses loader, just uncertainty = TRUE. slots paper treats random become moon_param_* spec objects; everything else stays plain. slot shapes match deterministic loader, contents uncertain slots changed: mortality_hr$OW ($OB1, $OB2) list-column moon_param_lognormal specs, one per age band. cost_df$cost list-column moon_param_gamma specs, one per (age, state) cell. Ages 2–19 mean = se = 0 (degenerate gammas always sample 0). transition_probs becomes list(specs, bands) — specs holds 18 moon_param_mvnorm objects (one per transition × age band). init_prev, qx, scalars, cohort_n stay plain values: paper treat uncertain. store_traces controls much keep: "none" — discard per-iteration cohort traces. Smallest object; enough forest plots. "summary" (default) — keep iteration-level summary metrics . "" — keep every iteration’s full moon_deterministic. Required incremental_cost_age band plot. Uses ~-hundred KB per iteration. moon_psa return six slots: $summary — aggregated stats (sex, metric, mean, lower95, upper95, sd). Twelve metrics per sex. $per_iter — long table (iter, sex, metric, value) — source custom credible-interval calculation. $traces — depends store_traces. $draws — long table HR sampled values keyed (iter, parameter). Audit trail “exact draws produced iteration’s results”. $params_spec — input spec, can re-run scratch. $meta — n_iter, seed, parallel, runtime_sec, correlation flags, store_traces, tp_overrides. plot.moon_psa() two types: forest incremental_cost_age  Point estimate + 95% CI every metric.  Per-age incremental cost vs NW mean 2.5–97.5% bands across iterations. Requires store_traces = "". “’re locked ” message §8 — $per_iter long data frame one row per (iter × metric × sex), quantile-based summary one base-R call. seed — sets random seed sampling. seed + spec ⇒ bit-identical PSA results. correlate_hr / correlate_cost — toggle legacy single-Z / single-U shared-randomness behaviour. Default TRUE matches paper authors ran. Set FALSE independent draws per spec. parallel = TRUE — opt furrr (installed). Iterations parallelise trivially; skipped demo renders deterministically. store_traces — set "summary" "none" big runs; saved object can otherwise grow hundreds MB 1000 iterations.

title: Getting started with MOON
text: seed — sets random seed sampling. seed + spec ⇒ bit-identical PSA results. correlate_hr / correlate_cost — toggle legacy single-Z / single-U shared-randomness behaviour. Default TRUE matches paper authors ran. Set FALSE independent draws per spec. parallel = TRUE — opt furrr (installed). Iterations parallelise trivially; skipped demo renders deterministically. store_traces — set "summary" "none" big runs; saved object can otherwise grow hundreds MB 1000 iterations.

title: Getting started with MOON
text: Pass tp_overrides= moon_psa() override applied every iteration. gives credible interval scenario quantity — SA2 (eliminate OB1+OB2), YLL distribution. seed runs ⇒ paired draws ⇒ YLL distribution within-iteration difference, difference independent samples — want credible interval incremental quantity.
code: psa_F_base <- moon_psa(   spec_F,   n_iter = 100,   seed = 123,   store_traces = "none" ) psa_F_sa2 <- moon_psa(   spec_F,   n_iter = 100,   seed = 123,   tp_overrides = list(set_zero = "OW_OB1", init_prev = ip_sa2),   store_traces = "none" )  le_base <- psa_F_base$per_iter$value[psa_F_base$per_iter$metric == "LE"] le_sa2 <- psa_F_sa2$per_iter$value[psa_F_sa2$per_iter$metric == "LE"] yll <- le_sa2 - le_base  sprintf(   "Female YLL from eliminating OB1+OB2: %.2f y [%.2f, %.2f]",   mean(yll),   quantile(yll, 0.025),   quantile(yll, 0.975) ) [1] "Female YLL from eliminating OB1+OB2: 1.33 y [1.07, 1.62]"

title: Getting started with MOON
text: “Customizing MOON” — companion vignette covering alter input (deterministic PSA), swap datasets, build PSA specs scratch. ?moon_deterministic, ?moon_psa, ?moon_params_norway — function--function reference. tests/testthat/ — anchor values deterministic PSA paths; reference .rds files ground truth build must reproduce 1e-10. Bjørnelv et al. 2021 — doi:10.1177/0272989X20971589. supplementary appendix per-band transition tables cost-model structure.

title: Modeling Obesity in Norway (MOON) Markov Cohort Model
text: moon R implementation MOON (Modeling Obesity Norway) Markov cohort model originally published Bjørnelv et al. (2021). simulates obesity prevalence, incremental health-care costs, years life lost Norwegian birth cohort life course, supports deterministic runs 1000-iteration probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) published correlated-draw conventions mortality hazard ratios, costs, transition probabilities. Status: early development. moon active early development. public API may change without deprecation warnings first tagged release, outputs verified expectations (see Disclaimer).

title: Modeling Obesity in Norway (MOON) Markov Cohort Model
text: probabilistic uncertainty, use spec’d parameter list moon_psa(): PSA call materialises 1000 parameter draws via moon_sample_params() runs moon_deterministic() . See ?moon_psa correlation flags store_traces options.
code: library(moon)  # 1. Build a Norwegian default `params` list (single sex per call). params <- moon_params_norway(sex = "female")  # 2. Run the deterministic simulation. run <- moon_deterministic(params)  run #> <moon_deterministic> #>   Sex:         female (cohort N = 26,458) #>   Horizon:     ages 2 to 100 #>   LE:          81.49 years #>   Total cost:  EUR 3,090,827,645 undisc / EUR 435,536,655 disc (r = 4%) #>   Per-capita inc cost vs NW (undisc): EUR 16,105  # 3. Inspect costs and prevalence. moon_costs(run, by = "state") #>      state       cost #> 1 N_always  584363548 #> 2   N_prev  307381370 #> 3      OB1  815463456 #> 4      OB2  334218808 #> 5       OW 1049400463 moon_prevalence(run, ages = 45) #>   age    state prevalence #> 1  45 N_always 0.28539951 #> 2  45   N_prev 0.10935520 #> 4  45      OB1 0.15295536 #> 5  45      OB2 0.06166763 #> 3  45       OW 0.39062230 spec <- moon_params_norway(sex = "female", uncertainty = TRUE) psa <- moon_psa(spec, n_iter = 1000, seed = 1)  summary(psa) plot(psa) # forest plot of headline metrics

title: Modeling Obesity in Norway (MOON) Markov Cohort Model
text: Running moon bundled Norwegian parameters produces results differ slightly reported Bjørnelv et al. (2021), due minor improvements made model transition original Excel/VBA implementation R. improvements : Mortality–cycle alignment. Baseline life-table mortality aligned cycle uses mortality probability age cohort occupies start cycle (cycle 1, advancing age 2 3, uses q_2). Survival-conditional transitions. State transitions applied individuals survive cycle, mortality BMI-state transitions modelled sequential rather independent events within cycle. Consistent survival functions across sex strata. parametric survival distribution used transition age band across female, male, combined-sex models.

what: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
title: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
text: Runs MOON Markov cohort model supplied parameters, returning tidy moon_deterministic object containing per-age state trace, per-age per-state costs, validated parameters, run metadata.

title: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
code: moon_deterministic(   params,   tp_overrides = NULL,   strict = TRUE,   record_meta = TRUE )

title: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
text: params params list returned moon_params_norway(). Must pass moon_check_params() strict = TRUE. tp_overrides Optional named list overrides applied engine entry. Supported slots: set_zero — character vector transitions zero across ages, e.g. "OW_OB1". init_prev — replaces initial-state vector. start_age — shifts cohort entry age. engine selects rows transition_probs positional index, callers shifting start_age must also supply correspondingly subset transition_probs / qx / cost_df. strict Passed moon_check_params(); TRUE (default) halts validation failure. record_meta Logical, default TRUE. FALSE returned meta list reduced fields downstream methods need (iter, seed, horizon, discount_rate, tp_overrides) per-call moon_version / run_time / duration_sec / cycle_length fields skipped. moon_psa() sets FALSE strip percent per-iteration overhead; ordinary single-run callers leave TRUE.

title: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
text: moon_deterministic S3 object — list trace — long data frame (age, sex, state, n) head-counts across six engine states (N_always, N_prev, OW, OB1, OB2, dead). costs — long data frame (age, sex, state, cost, cost_disc) total annual cohort costs state, cost_disc discounted params$discount_rate. dead rows. params — validated input parameters. meta — run metadata (timestamps, horizon, discount rate, etc.).

title: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
text: Single-sex per call: sex label read names(params$cohort_n) (one "female", "male", ""). sex-stratified results, run twice concatenate trace / costs rows downstream.

title: Run a deterministic MOON simulation — moon_deterministic
text: MOON's engine internally distinguishes two normal-weight states: N_always (currently NW, never previously OW OB) N_prev (currently NW previously overweight obese earlier age). conflated user-facing init_prev parameter, 4-vector c(NW, OW, OB1, OB2). Default mapping: NW initial mass placed N_always N_prev starts 0. states evolve independently per engine's six-state transition matrix; mass flows N_prev OW / OB1 / OB2 individuals regress NW simulation. reflects published MOON model (Bjørnelv et al. 2021), Norwegian birth cohort enters age 2 obesity history. Overriding default: separate init_n_always_share argument. Instead, supply full six-element initial-state vector via tp_overrides$init_prev; engine uses verbatim skips 4--6 mapping. Example, hypothetical cohort whose NW members previously overweight:   Pairing start_age: init_prev BMI distribution cohort entry age. shift entry age — mutating params$start_age passing tp_overrides$start_age — also supply matching init_prev (mutate params$init_prev former, pass tp_overrides$init_prev latter), cohort enter new age wrong BMI distribution. validator catch . See vignette("moon-customizing", package = "moon") §6 bootstrap recipe.
code: tp_overrides = list(   init_prev = c(N_always = 0.5, N_prev = 0.4,                 OW = 0.07, OB1 = 0.02, OB2 = 0.01, D = 0) )

title: Build a Norwegian default params list — moon_params_norway
text: Constructs engine-shape params list published MOON Norwegian birth cohort (Bjørnelv et al. 2021), reading bundled CSVs life tables, costs, survival-model coefficients. returned list plugs straight moon_deterministic() (moon_sample_params() / moon_psa() uncertainty = TRUE) reshape.

title: Build a Norwegian default params list — moon_params_norway
text: sex One "female", "male", "". Selects CSV file family load life-table column use qx. uncertainty FALSE (default), returns plain numeric values ready moon_deterministic(). TRUE, wraps uncertain inputs (mortality hazard ratios, costs, transition coefficients) moon_param_* spec objects ready moon_sample_params() / moon_psa(). result valid input moon_deterministic() directly — sample first. data_dir Directory containing parameter CSVs. Defaults system.file("extdata", package = "moon", mustWork = TRUE), resolves bundled files inside installed package; override point custom directory CSVs filenames schema. lifetable_file File name (within data_dir) life-table CSV. Defaults "Lifetable_Norway_2017.csv" match bundled data; override supplying life table different country / year. CSV must Age column sex-keyed column matching sex argument (F, M, ).

title: Build a Norwegian default params list — moon_params_norway
text: params list elements start_age, max_age, discount_rate, cost_currency, cohort_n, init_prev, qx, mortality_hr, transition_probs, cost_df. uncertainty = TRUE shape, uncertain slots replaced moon_param_* specs: mortality_hr$OW / OB1 / OB2 become list-columns moon_param_lognormal() specs. cost_df$cost becomes list moon_param_gamma() specs (one per (state, age) cell; degenerate ages 2–19 get gammas mean = se = 0 always sample 0). transition_probs becomes list $specs (named moon_param_mvnorm() objects keyed "<transition>_<age_start>_<age_end>") $bands (schema stitching per-band draws back deterministic transition_probs layout).

code: moon_psa(   spec,   n_iter,   seed = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   store_traces = c("summary", "all", "none"),   correlate_hr = TRUE,   correlate_cost = TRUE,   tp_overrides = NULL )

title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) on a MOON spec — moon_psa
text: spec spec'd params list, typically moon_params_norway(sex = ..., uncertainty = TRUE). n_iter Integer; number PSA iterations. published MOON analysis uses 1000. seed Integer (NULL); passed moon_sample_params() draw sequence reproducible. parallel Logical. FALSE (default) runs iterations sequentially lapply. TRUE uses furrr::future_map multicore (Linux / macOS) multisession (Windows) plan; falls back sequential warning furrr / future unavailable. store_traces One "summary" (default — keep 3-D array [iter, age, state] trace counts), "" (keep full list moon_deterministic objects; large), "none" (drop traces; keep summary, per-iter metrics, draws). correlate_hr, correlate_cost Logical, passed moon_sample_params(); default TRUE. tp_overrides Optional, forwarded every moon_deterministic() call. Use run PSA scenario rather base case.

title: Run a probabilistic sensitivity analysis (PSA) on a MOON spec — moon_psa

title: Materialise a spec'd params list into n concrete draws — moon_sample_params
text: Two correlation flags reproduce published MOON conventions: correlate_hr (default TRUE) — one rnorm(n) draw reused Z-source 9 mortality-HR lognormals (3 states × 3 age bands). correlate_cost (default TRUE) — one runif(n) draw reused inverse-CDF input every cost gamma. flag FALSE, spec generates independent draws. Within single call (fixed seed) RNG order HR z → cost u → transition-coefficient mvnorm draws (order band-key). order stable across calls expected match legacy MOON port byte--byte.

what: Run the raw MOON Markov engine — run_markov_engine
title: Run the raw MOON Markov engine — run_markov_engine
text: Low-level pure-function entry point MOON Markov cohort engine. users call moon_deterministic() instead, builds validates inputs returns tidy output. run_markov_engine() exported advanced sensitivity-analysis workflows need direct access engine's six-state trace per-capita cost matrix.

title: Run the raw MOON Markov engine — run_markov_engine
code: run_markov_engine(   start_age,   max_age,   discount_rate,   init_prev,   transition_probs,   qx,   mortality_hr,   cost_df,   tp_overrides = NULL )

title: Run the raw MOON Markov engine — run_markov_engine
text: start_age Integer; first model age (e.g. 2). max_age Integer; one past last model age (e.g. 100). n_cycles = max_age - start_age. discount_rate Numeric [0, 1); applied cost_matrix . init_prev Named numeric c(NW = ..., OW = ..., OB1 = ..., OB2 = ...) summing 1. NW mass enters engine N_always; N_prev starts 0. transition_probs Data frame columns age, NW_OW, OW_OB1, OB1_OB2, OW_NW, OB1_OW, OB2_OB1 — one row per age start_age:(max_age - 1). qx Named numeric vector NW baseline mortality probabilities; names ages character. mortality_hr Data frame columns age_lower, OW, OB1, OB2 (three rows, one per age band 35 / 50 / 70). cost_df Data frame columns age, state (NW / OW / OB1 / OB2), cost. Single-sex, already filtered. NULL zero-row costs. tp_overrides Optional list. Supported slots: set_zero (character vector transitions zero ), init_prev (overrides init_prev), start_age (overrides start_age).

title: Run the raw MOON Markov engine — run_markov_engine
text: list trace — (n_cycles + 1) x 6 matrix state proportions (rows sum 1). cost_matrix — (n_cycles + 1) x 4 matrix per-capita costs (age, state). mortality — list mortality probability vectors per state (NW, OW, OB1, OB2).

title: Run the raw MOON Markov engine — run_markov_engine
text: engine pure: identical inputs produce byte-identical outputs. /O, global state, set.seed() calls.

title: moon 0.1.1
text: Add citation infrastructure now package archived Zenodo: CITATION.cff, inst/CITATION (citation("moon") returns software citation Zenodo concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20842690), DOI badge plus software citation README.

title: moon 0.1.0
text: First public release MOON Markov cohort model. Public API: moon_params_norway() builds Norwegian default parameter set (spec’d uncertainty version); moon_deterministic() runs single deterministic simulation; moon_psa() runs probabilistic sensitivity analysis. S3 methods (print, summary, plot, .data.frame) extractors (moon_prevalence(), moon_costs()) cover routine inspection results. Bundled package data: moon_who_cutoffs (adult BMI cut-offs) moon_iotf_cutoffs (Cole/IOTF child BMI cut-offs, ages 2–18 0.5-year steps).
